name: TestJoinPDUEvent
title: TestJoinPDUEvent
syntax: long TestJoinPDUEvent (dbPDU aPDU, dword flags1); // form 1
long TestJoinPDUEvent (char aPDUName[], dword flags2); // form 2
long TestJoinPDUEvent (dword aHeaderID, dword flags3); // form 3
description: Completes the current set of joined events with the transmitted event. This function does not wait.

parameters.name: aPDU
parameters.description: PDU to be awaited as it is defined in the database.Default value: wait for any PDU.

parameters.name: aPDUName
parameters.description: Name of a PDU to be awaited as it is defined in the database. Possibly the TX node’s name can be given as a prefix, e.g. <TXNodeName>::<PDUName>.

parameters.name: Note If the header ID is not unique, the function will return on the PDU that is first found in the database. In those cases it is better to use the PDU name.
flags3
parameters.description: The lowest bit denotes whether the long or short header ID is to be resolved: 0: the short Header-ID is used. 1: the long header ID is used. All other bits are reserved and should be set to 0.

parameters.name: flags1, flags2, flags4
parameters.description: Reserved and should be set to 0.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
